title: Quick weeknight lasagne
sources: Pellegrino Artusi, La Scienza in Cucina (1891) — reference for the traditional version this weeknight one is shortened from.
Anglo-American household-magazine adaptations of lasagne, c.1950s-1970s, give the weeknight pattern of passata-based meat sauce and béchamel built straight in the pan.
notes: Counterpart to lasagne-alla-bolognese: 45 minutes start to finish, passata in place of a slow ragù, quick béchamel built into the pan. Same shape but a different week. Reference the slow version as the deep dive; this one is the Tuesday-night dinner.
